task_completion.description: Create a collection named 'AI History Seminar'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a folder or collection named 'AI History Seminar' in the left pane of the reference manager (Zotero).

task_completion.description: Add the four specified historical AI papers to the collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'AI History Seminar' collection selected, the middle pane should display the four papers: Turing (1950), Turing (1936), Shannon (1948), and McCarthy (1960).

task_completion.description: Add a child note to 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a note item nested directly under the 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' paper in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Write the specified text in the child note
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the right pane (note editor) to verify it contains the exact text: 'Essential reading: Discusses the Imitation Game.'

task_completion.description: Generate a report from the 'AI History Seminar' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a generated report window or tab (usually triggered by right-clicking the collection and selecting 'Generate Report from Collection...').

task_completion.description: Save the generated report as an HTML file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog saving the file as 'ai_seminar_report.html' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager showing this file exists.

integrity.description: The agent used the reference manager (Zotero) GUI to organize the papers and notes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Zotero application window, not just terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in report generation feature
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Zotero 'Generate Report' interface being used, rather than the agent manually typing out an HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by directly creating a fake HTML file
integrity.visual_evidence: The final HTML file should be a genuine Zotero report export (identifiable by its specific formatting and layout), not a manually crafted HTML document in a text editor.
